title: Developer shrinks Syracuse apartment project near I-81 for second time
summary: Developer Christopher Geiger has reduced the Gateway Apartments project near I-81 for the second time, cutting units from 291 to 259 (11% reduction) due to ineligibility for expected state brownfield tax credits on the eastern portion of the site. The seven-story project at East Fayette and Almond streets remains on track as the first development along the Almond Street corridor post-I-81 demolition, though no groundbreaking is expected in 2025 pending investor financing.

political_signal.description: Project scaling represents adaptive management of I-81 corridor redevelopment strategy; developer pivoting based on state tax credit eligibility demonstrates market-driven iteration on flagship infrastructure replacement initiative.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts extracted ($129.8M project cost, $24M tax break request) validated in source text
